q:
A series is given, with one term missing. Choose the correct alternative from the given ones that will complete the 
series. 
WYV, ? , IKH, BDA
explanation: Expression : WYV, ? , IKH, BDA The pattern followed is that there is a gap of six letters between each letters in every term. 1 st letter : W (-7 letters) = P (-7 letters) = I (-7 letters) = B 2 nd letter : Y (-7 letters) = R (-7 letters) = K (-7 letters) = D 3 rd letter : V (-7 letters) = O (-7 letters) = H (-7 letters) = A Thus, missing term = PRO

subject: Reasoning Ability
question: A is B's brother, C is A's mother, D is C's father, F is A's son. How is A related to F's child?
q: A is B's brother, C is A's mother, D is C's father, F is A's son. How is A related to F's child?
explanation: Note :- The first three statements are redundant. From the last statement, F is the son of A. Thus, F's child is the grandchild of A. => A is the grandfather of F's child

subject: Reasoning Ability
question: Seven boys A,B,C,D,E,F and G are standing in a line. (i) G is between A and E (ii) F and A have one boy between them (iii) E and C have two boys between them (iv) D is immediate right of F (v) C and B have three boys between them Who is second from left?
q:
Seven boys A,B,C,D,E,F and G are standing in a line. 
(i) G is between A and E 
(ii) F and A have one boy between them 
(iii) E and C have two boys between them 
(iv) D is immediate right of F 
(v) C and B have three boys between 
them 
Who is second from left?
explanation: G is between A and E, => Let us take E to the left of G and A to the right of G. F and A have one boy between them, => F is second to the right of A (∵ E is second to the left). E and C have two boys between them, => C is between A and F (∵ there are only 7 boys). D is immediate right of F and C and B have three boys between them, => B is to the extreme left. ∴ Arrangement = B E G A C F D Thus, E is second from the left.

question: Pranav went 10 Km to the North. Then he turned West and covered 10 Km. Then he turned south and covered 5 Km. Finally, he turned to the East and covered 10 Km. In which direction he is from the starting point?
q:
Pranav went 10 Km to the North. Then he turned West and covered 10 Km. Then he turned south and covered 5 Km. Finally, he 
turned to the East and covered 10 Km. In which direction he is from the starting point?
explanation: Let Pranav started form point E and walked towards point A for 10 km, then he turned left and walked for 10 km to reach B, then again he turned left and travelled south for 5 km, and finally stopped at point D after walking 10 km. Thus, he is in north direction from starting point.

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: The lengths of diagonals of a rhombus are 24 cm and 10 cm. The perimeter of the rhombus (in cm) is:
q: The lengths of diagonals of a rhombus are 24 cm and 10 cm. The perimeter of the rhombus (in cm) is:
explanation: The diagonals of a rhombus bisect each other at right angles. Let$ABCD$be the rhombus with diagonals intersecting at$O. *OB = \frac{24}{2} = 12\text{ cm}*OA = \frac{10}{2} = 5\text{ cm}In right-angledtriangle OAB:AB^2 = OA^2 + OB^2 AB^2 = 5^2 + 12^2 =25 + 144 = 169 AB= \sqrt{169} = 13 \text{ cm}Perimeter of the rhombus:\text{Perimeter} = 4 \times AB = 4 \times 13 = 52\text{ cm}$.

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: A dishonest shopkeeper professes to sell goods at h is cost price but uses a false weight of 950 gms, for each kilogram. H is gain percentage is
q:
A dishonest shopkeeper professes to sell goods at his cost price but uses a false weight of 950 gms, for each kilogram. His 
 gain percentage is
explanation: The dealer gives$875\text{ g}$instead $ of 1000\text{ g}. Profit on cost weight:\text{Profit \%} = \frac{\text{Error}}{\text{True Weight} - \text{Error}} \times 100= \frac{1000 - 950}{950} \times 100= \frac{50}{950} \times 100= \frac{1}{19} \times 100 = 5\frac{5}{19}\%$.

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: Ratio of A's age to B's age is$4: 3$. 'A' will be 26 years old after 6 years. The age of B now is
q: Ratio of A's age to B's age is$4:3$. 'A' will be 26 years old after 6 years. The age of B now is
explanation: It is given that A will be 26 years old after 6 years $=> A's present age =26 - 6 = 20 years$ Also, ratio of A : B present ages =$4: 3$=> B's present age = 3 4 $\times =3 \times 5 = 15 years$

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: A man sells an article at 15% profit. If he had sold it for ₹ 6 more, he would have gained 18%. The man bought the article for
q: A man sells an article at 15% profit. If he had sold it for ₹ 6 more, he would have gained 18%. The man bought the article for
explanation: Let the cost price of the article be$100 x. * Selling Price (SP) at 15\% profit= 115 x. $According to the question:(115 x + 6) - 100 x = 18\% \text{ of } 100 x 15 x + 6 = 18 x 3 x = 6 \implies x = 2 Therefore, the cost price is:\text{Cost Price} = 100 $\times$ 2 = \text{Rs. } 200.

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: A's salary was decreased by 50% and subsequently increased by 50%. How much percent does he lose?
q: A's salary was decreased by 50% and subsequently increased by 50%. How much percent does he lose?
explanation: Let A's initial salary be 100. $* After decreasing by 50\%, new salary= 50$. * After increasing by 50% of the new salary, final salary=$50 + 50% \text{ of } 50 =50 + 25 = 75. Net loss percentage is:\frac{100 - 75}{100} times 100 = 25\%$.

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: A car moving in the morning fog passes a man walking at 4 km/h. in the same direction. The man can see the car for 3 minutes and visibility is upto a distance of 130 m. The speed of the car is :
q:
A car moving in the morning fog passes a man walking at 4km/h. in the same direction. The man can see the car for 3 minutes 
and visibility is upto a distance of 130m. The speed of the car is :
explanation:
Let the speed of the car be $s$ km/h and the speed of the man be $4$ km/h. Relative speed $= (s - 4)$ km/h. The man can see the car for $3$ minutes $\left(\frac{3}{60} = \frac{1}{20}\text{ hours}\right)$ up to a distance of $130$ meters ($0.13$ km):
$$\text{Speed} = \frac{\text{Distance}}{\text{Time}}$$
$$s - 4 = \frac{0.13}{\frac{1}{20}}$$
$$s - 4 = 2.6 \implies s = 6.6 = 6\frac{3}{5}\text{ km/h}.$$

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: A money lender lends Rs. 400 for 3 years to a person and lends Rs. 500 for 4 years to the other person at the same rate of simple interest. If altogether he receives Rs. 160 as interest, what is the rate of interest per annum ?
q:
A money lender lends Rs. 400 for 3 years to a person and lends Rs. 500 for 4 years to the other person at the same rate of 
simple interest. If altogether he receives Rs. 160 as interest, what is the rate of interest per annum ?
explanation: Let the rate of interest be$r\%. $* Interest from first person= $\frac{400 \times r \times 3}{100}$ = 12 r* Interest from second person= $\frac{500 \times r \times 4}{100}$ = 20 r Total interest: 12 r + 20 r = 16032 r = 160 \implies r = 5\%.

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: If x $= \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}+1}$, then the value of $x^2 + 2 x - 1 $ is:
q: If x $= \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}+1}$, then the value of $x^2 + 2 x - 1 $ is:
explanation: $Rationalizingx:x = \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}+1} \times \frac{\sqrt{2}-1}{\sqrt{2}-1} = \frac{\sqrt{2}-1}{2-1} = \sqrt{2}-1 x + 1 = \sqrt{2}Squaring$ both $sides:(x + 1)^2 = (\sqrt{2})^2 x^2 + 2 x + 1 = 2 x^2 + 2 x - 1 = 0$.

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: If x $+ \frac{1}{x} = \sqrt{13}$, then what is the value of $\frac{3 x}{x^2-1}$?
q: If x $+ \frac{1}{x} = \sqrt{13}$, then what is the value of $\frac{3 x}{x^2-1}$?
explanation: Given:$x + \frac{1}{x} = \sqrt{13}Squaring both sides:left(x + \frac{1}{x}\right)^2 = 13 x^2 + \frac{1}{x^2} + 2 = 13 \implies x^2 + \frac{1}{x^2} = 11 \quad \text{---(i)}Also:left(x - \frac{1}{x}\right)^2 = x^2 + \frac{1}{x^2} - 2 left(x - \frac{1}{x}\right)^2 =11 - 2 = 9 x - \frac{1}{x} = 3 \frac{x^2 - 1}{x} = 3 \implies x^2 - 1 = 3 x Therefore:\frac{3 x}{x^2 - 1}= \frac{3 x}{3 x} = 1$.

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: O is the centre of a circle. P is an external point at a distance of 13 cm from O. The radius of the circle is 5 cm. Then the length of a tangent to the circle from P up to the point of contact is:
q: O is the centre of a circle. P is an external point at a distance of 13 cm from O. The radius of the circle is 5 cm. Then the length of a tangent to the circle from P up to the point of contact is:
explanation: The radius of a circle intersects the tangent at a right angle, soangle OTP $= 90^\circwhere T $ is the point of contact. In $right-angledtriangle OPT:PT^2 = OP^2 - OT^2 PT^2 = 13^2 - 5^2 =169 - 25 = 144 PT= \sqrt{144} = 12$ \text{ cm}.

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: Ifthetais an acute angle $andsin(\theta+18^\circ) = \frac{1}{2}$, then the value ofthetain circular measure is:
q: Ifthetais an acute angle $andsin(\theta+18^\circ) = \frac{1}{2}$, then the value ofthetain circular measure is:
explanation:
Given: $\sin(\theta + 18^\circ) = \frac{1}{2} \implies \sin(\theta + 18^\circ) = \sin 30^\circ \implies \theta + 18^\circ = 30^\circ \implies \theta = 12^\circ$. Converting $\theta$ to radians:
$$\theta = 12^\circ \times \frac{\pi}{180^\circ} = \frac{\pi}{15}\text{ radians}.$$

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: 18 men or 36 boys working 6 hours a day can plough a field in 24 days. In how many days will 24 men and 24 boys working 9 hours a day plough the same field?
q: 18 men or 36 boys working 6 hours a day can plough a field in 24 days. In how many days will 24 men and 24 boys working 9 hours a day plough the same field?
explanation: 18 men$equiv$36 boys$implies 1\text{ man} \equiv 2\text{ boys}$. Therefore, $24 men and 24 boys is equivalent to: 24 \times 2 + 24 = 72 \text{ boys}Using the work formula M_1 D_1 H_1 = M_2 D_2 H_2: 36\text{ boys} \times 24\text{ days} \times 6\text{ hours} = 72\text{ boys} \times D_2 \times 9\text{ hours}36 \times 24 \times 6 = 72 \times D_2 \times 924 \times 3 = 9 \times D_2 \implies 72 = 9 D_2 \implies D_2 = 8\text{ days}$.

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: If a(x $+$ y) = b(x - y) = $2$ a b, then the value of $2(x^2 + y^2)$ is:
q: If a(x $+$ y) = b(x - y) = $2$ a b, then the value of $2(x^2 + y^2)$ is:
explanation: Given:$a(x + y) = 2 a b \implies x + y = 2 bb(x - y) = 2 a b \implies x - y = 2 a Squaring both equations:(x + y)^2 = 4 b^2 \implies x^2 + y^2 + 2 xy = 4 b^2(x - y)^2 = 4 a^2 \implies x^2 + y^2 - 2 xy = 4 a^2 Adding these two equations: 2(x^2 + y^2) = 4(a^2 + b^2)$.

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: If x $+ \frac{1}{x} =$ 6, then the value of $x^2 + \frac{1}{x^2}$ is:
q: If x $+ \frac{1}{x} =$ 6, then the value of $x^2 + \frac{1}{x^2}$ is:
explanation: Given:$x + \frac{1}{x} = 6 Squaring both sides:left(x + \frac{1}{x}\right)^2 = 6^2 x^2 + \frac{1}{x^2} + 2 = 36 x^2 + \frac{1}{x^2} = 34$.

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: G is the centroid of the equilateral triangle ABC. If AB $=$ 9cm, then AG is equal to:
q: G is the centroid of the equilateral triangle ABC. If AB $=$ 9cm, then AG is equal to:
explanation: In an equilateral triangle with sidea $= 9cm: * Median/Altitude AD = \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}$ a = $\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} \times 9 = 4.5\sqrt{3}cm Since G $ is the centroid, it divides the median AD in the ratio $2$: $1$ from the $vertex:AG = \frac{2}{3}$ AD = $\frac{2}{3} \times \frac{9\sqrt{3}}{2} = 3\sqrt{3}$ cm .

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: In the figure (not drawn to scale) given below, if AD $=$ DC = BC andangle BCE = $96^\circ$, thenangle $DBC $ is:
q: In the figure (not drawn to scale) given below, if AD $=$ DC = BC andangle BCE = $96^\circ$, thenangle $DBC $ is:
explanation: Letangle CAD $= x. *$ Since AD = DC,angle ACD = $\angle$ CAD = $x. *$ The exterior angleangle CDB = $\angle$ CAD + $\angle$ ACD = $2 x. *$ Since DC = BC,angle CBD $= \angle$ CDB = $2 x. *$ In triangle BCD,angle BCD = $180^\circ - 4 x. *$ The angles around the straight line $at C are:angle$ ACD + $\angle$ BCD + $\angle$ BCE = $180^\circx + (180^\circ - 4$ x) + $96^\circ = 180^\circ 276^\circ - 3 x = 180^\circ \implies 3 x = 96^\circ \implies x = 32^\circ Therefore:angle$ DBC = $2 x = 64^\circ$.

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: Ifthetais a positive acute angle and $4\s in^2 \theta =$ 3, then the value oftan $\theta - \cot \frac{\theta}{2}$ is:
q: Ifthetais a positive acute angle and $4\s in^2 \theta =$ 3, then the value oftan $\theta - \cot \frac{\theta}{2}$ is:
explanation: Given:$4\s in^2 \theta = 3 \implies \s in^2 \theta = \frac{3}{4} \implies \s in \theta = \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}Sincethetais acute:theta = 60^\circ Now, substitutingtheta = 60^\circ:tan \theta - \cot \frac{\theta}{2} = \tan 60^\circ - \cot 30^\circ = \sqrt{3} - \sqrt{3} = 0$.

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: The chord$AB$of a circle of centre$O$subtends an angle$theta$with the tangent at$A$to the circle. Then the measure $ of angle ABO$is:
q: The chord$AB$of a circle of centre$O$subtends an angle$theta$with the tangent at$A$to the circle. Then the measure $ of angle ABO$is:
explanation: Let AC be the tangent at A to the circle with centre O. The radius OA is perpendicular to the tangent AC, soangle OAC $= 90^\circ. *$ Givenangle BAC $= \theta. *$ Thus,angle OAB $= 90^\circ - \theta. Since OA =$ OB(radii of the same circle),triangle OAB is an isosceles $triangle:angle$ ABO = $\angle$ OAB = $90^\circ - \theta$.

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: From a point on a bridge across the river, the angles of depression of the banks on opposite sides of the river are 30° and 45° respectively. If the bridge is at a height of 2.5 m from the banks, then the width of the river is: $(take \sqrt{3} =$ 1.732)
q: From a point on a bridge across the river, the angles of depression of the banks on opposite sides of the river are 30° and 45° respectively. If the bridge is at a height of 2.5 m from the banks, then the width of the river is: $(take \sqrt{3} =$ 1.732)
explanation: Let the bridge be at point A at height AC $= 2.5\text{$ m}above the river bed BD. The banks on opposite sides are B and D. In $right-angledtriangle ACD:tan 45^\circ = \frac{AC}{CD} \implies 1 = \frac{2.5}{CD} \implies$ CD $= 2.5\text{$ m}In $right-angledtriangle ACB:tan 30^\circ = \frac{AC}{BC} \implies \frac{1}{\sqrt{3}} = \frac{2.5}{BC} \implies$ BC $= 2.5\sqrt{3} =$ 2.5 $\times$ 1.732 = $4.33\text{$ m}Width of the $river:BD =$ BC + CD $=4.33 +$ 2.5 $=$ 6.83 \text{ m}.

subject: General Awareness
question: Which of the following green house gases has the greatest heat-trapping ability?
q: Which of the following green house gases has the greatest heat-trapping ability?
explanation: Chlorofluorocarbons (CFC s) are extremely potent greenhouse gases with heat-trapping abilities thousands of times greater than carbon dioxide.

subject: General Awareness
question: Which State of India is leading in solar energy generation ?
q:
Which State of India is leading in solar energy 
generation ?
explanation: Rajasthan is leading in solar energy generation in India due to its vast desert areas and high solar irradiance.

subject: General Awareness
question: Diamond does not conduct electricity, because
q: Diamond does not conduct electricity, because

options.text: There are only carbon atoms present in it
No free electrons are present in it

explanation: Diamond does not conduct electricity because all its valence electrons are tightly bound in covalent bonds, leaving no free electrons to conduct current.

subject: General Awareness
question: Ganga is a result of confluence of rivers Bhagirathi and Alakananda at which place ?
q: Ganga is a result of confluence of rivers Bhagirathi and Alakananda at which place ?
explanation: The Bhagirathi and Alaknanda rivers meet at Dev Prayag in Uttarakhand to form the holy Ganges river.

subject: General Awareness
question: What are Aldehydes ?
q: What are Aldehydes ?
explanation: Aldehydes are organic compounds containing a functional group with the structure -CHO, which acts as a mild reducing agent.

subject: General Awareness
question: Which circuit is used to store one bit of data ?
q: Which circuit is used to store one bit of data ?
explanation: A flip-flop is a basic electronic circuit component used to store a single bit of binary data (0 or 1).

subject: General Awareness
question: Why is weightlessness experienced while orbiting the earth in space ships ?
q: Why is weightlessness experienced while orbiting the earth in space ships ?
explanation: Weightlessness in an orbiting space ship is experienced because the ship and its occupants are in a state of continuous free fall towards Earth under gravity.

subject: General Awareness
question: Which is the largest gland in human body?
q: Which is the largest gland in human body?
explanation: The liver is the largest internal gland and organ in the human body, performing vital metabolic and detoxification functions.

subject: General Awareness
question: Who was the President of Indian National Congress at the time of Indian independence ?
q: Who was the President of Indian National Congress at the time of Indian independence ?
explanation: J. B. Kripalani was the President of the Indian National Congress when India achieved independence in August 1947.

subject: General Awareness
question: What is the minimum age for membership to Rajya Sabha ?
q: What is the minimum age for membership to Rajya Sabha ?
explanation: According to the Indian Constitution, the minimum age required for becoming a member of the Rajya Sabha is 30 years.

subject: General Awareness
question: What is an octroi ?
q: What is an octroi ?
explanation: Octroi is a local tax levied by municipal authorities on goods entering a town or city for consumption or sale.

subject: General Awareness
question: Sextant is an instrument used in which of the following ?
q: Sextant is an instrument used in which of the following ?
explanation: A sextant is a doubly reflecting navigation instrument used to measure the angle between two visible objects, primarily for celestial navigation.

subject: General Awareness

explanation: Jain temples at Khajuraho are located in Madhya Pradesh (built by Chandelas), whereas Konark is in Odisha, and Ujjain is in Madhya Pradesh.

subject: General Awareness
question: What is Gol Gumbadh ?
q: What is Gol Gumbadh ?
explanation: Gol Gumbaz is the mausoleum of Mohammed Adil Shah, the Sultan of Bijapur, located in Karnataka.

subject: General Awareness
question: Where is a transistor most likely to be found ?
q: Where is a transistor most likely to be found ?
explanation: A transistor is commonly found in hearing aids, wristwatches, and other microelectronic devices to amplify or switch signals.

subject: General Awareness
question: Which organ of human body secretes Insulin?
q: Which organ of human body secretes Insulin?
explanation: Insulin is a hormone secreted by the beta cells of the pancreas to regulate blood glucose levels.

subject: General Awareness
question: If a healthy freshwater fish is placed in salt water, what will be the expected consequence ?
q:
If a healthy freshwater fish is placed in salt water, what will be the expected 
consequence ?

options.text: The fish becomes dehydrated and dies
The fish becomes bloated and dies
The fish suffers from fungal or bacterial disease and dies
There is no observable effect on the fish provided there is sufficient food

explanation: Placing a freshwater fish in salt water causes water to leave its body cells by osmosis (exosmosis), leading to severe dehydration and death.

subject: General Awareness
question: Which country is known as 'L and of Midnight Sun'?
q: Which country is known as 'Land of Midnight Sun'?
explanation: Norway is called the 'L and of the Midnight Sun' because parts of the country lie north of the Arctic Circle where the sun remains visible at midnight in summer.

explanation: The Ganges river basin is the largest in India and is shared by 11 states, including Uttar Pradesh, Bihar, and West Bengal.

question: In the following question, one part of the sentence may have an error. Find out which part of the sentence has an error and click the button corresponding to it. q:
In the following questions, one part of the sentence may have an error. Find out which part of the sentence has an error and 
click the button corresponding to it. If the sentence is free from error, click the "No error" option. 
I have come (A)/ as soon as (B)/ the customers leave your shop (C)/ No Error (D)
explanation: The phrase 'I have come' (present perfect) is incorrect in the time clause. It should be 'I will come' or 'I come' to show future action.

subject: English Language

q:

sentence. Choose the correct alternative and click the button corresponding to it. In case no improvement is needed, click the 
button corresponding to "No improvement". 
I did not give at the examination as my circumstances are bad.

subject: English Language
question: A sentence/a part of the sentence is underlined. Four alternatives are given to the underlined part which will improve the sentence. Choose the correct alternative and click the button corresponding to it. In case no improvement is needed, click the button corresponding to "No improvement". Though he worked hard, but he failed.
q:
A sentence/a part of the sentence is underlined. Four alternatives are given to the underlined part which will improve the 
sentence. Choose the correct alternative and click the button corresponding to it. In case no improvement is needed, click the 
button corresponding to "No improvement". 
Though he worked hard, but he failed.
explanation: Use of "but" here is wrong cause Though is followed by ‘yet’. When though / although / even though is used with a verb, which is in the subjunctive mood, mainly expressing doubt, a condition contrary to a fact, a wish, or a concession, it should be followed by yet. Yet actually means still. Hence B

subject: English Language
question: A sentence/a part of the sentence is underlined. Four alternatives are given to the underlined part which will improve the sentence. Choose the correct alternative and click the button corresponding to it. In case no improvement is needed, click the button corresponding to "No improvement". No sooner did the teacher come into the class when we stood up.
q:
A sentence/a part of the sentence is underlined. Four alternatives are given to the underlined part which will improve the 
sentence. Choose the correct alternative and click the button corresponding to it. In case no improvement is needed, click the 
button corresponding to "No improvement". 
No sooner did the teacher come into the class when we stood up.
explanation: The combination of "did" with the "b are infinitive" is sometimes called an "emphatic" tense, and so the first sentence might be more "emphatic," calling attention to the fact that the teacher really entered the room. The expression ‘no sooner…than’ is used to suggest that one action or situation takes place/took place immediately after another action or situation. When ‘no sooner’ goes at the beginning of the sentence, we use an inverted word order. That means the auxiliary verb goes before the subject. Hence option C

Read the passage carefully and choose the best answer to each question out of the four alternatives and click the button corresponding to it. In the world today we make health an end in itself. We have forgotten that health really means to enable a person to do h is work and do it well. A lot of modern medicine, and this includes many patients as well as many physicians, pay very little attention to health but very much attention to those who imagine the y are ill. Our great concern with health is shown by the medical columns in newspapers, the health articles in popular magazines and the popularity of television programmes and all those books on medicine. We talk about health all the time. Yet for the most part the only result is more people with imaginary illness. A healthy man should not be wasting time talking about health : he should be using health for work.
